title: C4OS Grill Question 007: Runtime Backend
description: This question finalizes which runtime backend starts behind the thin C4OS adapter. Submit the form, then paste the generated response back into chat.

questions.label: Which runtime backend should the first implementation use?
options: OpenCode first behind a thin C4OS adapter
Pi first behind a thin C4OS adapter
Support both OpenCode and Pi from the start
questions.help: Your intake selected OpenCode first. The POCs found OpenCode stronger for server-backed sessions/events and Pi stronger for in-process approval interception.

questions.label: Why this backend first?
questions.help: Give the deciding product or implementation reason, especially considering concurrent runs, Browser/Terminal authority, and extension execution.

questions.label: What role should Pi have after choosing OpenCode or another backend?
options: Keep Pi as a later adapter target only
Use Pi for approval/tool-interception ideas but not runtime
Support Pi as a secondary runtime in MVP
questions.help: This prevents the product model from accidentally depending on both runtimes without a deliberate decision.

questions.label: Clarifications or constraints
questions.help: Add any requirements about server mode, event streams, provider config, extension events, cancellation, or local state isolation.
